children.text: The debate within a family from Panama’s elite, sparked by the populist referendum on the expansion of the Panama Canal, is illustrated through a poetic journey along a river, a coastline, and an interoceanic canal. Through this journey, the Panamanian documentary Familia explores whether what is understood as development serves human beings, or whether, on the contrary, human beings have been placed in service of the variables of a particular notion of development.
